quotes.quote: The drug candidates are ranked by an LLM-judged tournament according to the strength of the scientific rationale, pharmacological profile, and methodology of the supporting literature.
quotes.rationale: Identifies the LLM judge, its ranking role, target criteria, and literature basis.

quotes.quote: When comparing the LLM judge’s preferences with experts’, the LLM judge demonstrated high concordance with expert preferences, with an average of 7.25 of its top 10 hypotheses matching those in the experts’ top 10 (Supplementary Figure S11A). This concordance is more than double that expected from random selection. Furthermore, the LLM judge exhibited higher intra-rater consistency than human experts (Supplementary Figure S11B). When presented with identical pairwise comparisons, the LLM judge selected the same hypothesis in 88% of comparisons, as compared with human experts who selected the same hypothesis 61% of the time.
quotes.rationale: Reports agreement with experts and repeated-comparison consistency for the LLM judge.

quotes.quote: Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis. Robin then interprets the results of this analysis to generate a new round of therapeutic candidates.
quotes.rationale: Identifies Robin's analysis of supplied experimental data and routing into a new candidate round.

quotes.quote: Read demultiplexing and alignment was performed by a human and subsequent differential gene expression analysis was performed by Finch.
quotes.rationale: Separates human preprocessing from Finch differential gene expression analysis.

quotes.quote: Read demultiplexing and alignment was performed by a human and subsequent differential gene expression analysis was performed by Finch.
quotes.rationale: Identifies a human validator-like analysis step for read demultiplexing and alignment.

quotes.quote: Flow cytometry data was also analyzed by a human scientist.
quotes.rationale: Identifies human analysis of flow-cytometry data as a distinct validation channel.

diagnostics.consequence: Candidate ranking may be useful for ordering hypotheses, but the packet does not establish bias/error performance or calibration of the LLM judge beyond expert agreement and repeated-comparison consistency.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: The drug candidates are ranked by an LLM-judged tournament according to the strength of the scientific rationale, pharmacological profile, and methodology of the supporting literature.

When comparing the LLM judge’s preferences with experts’, the LLM judge demonstrated high concordance with expert preferences, with an average of 7.25 of its top 10 hypotheses matching those in the experts’ top 10 (Supplementary Figure S11A). This concordance is more than double that expected from random selection. Furthermore, the LLM judge exhibited higher intra-rater consistency than human experts (Supplementary Figure S11B). When presented with identical pairwise comparisons, the LLM judge selected the same hypothesis in 88% of comparisons, as compared with human experts who selected the same hypothesis 61% of the time.

diagnostics.statement: The LLM candidate-ranking channel has agreement evidence, not probabilistic calibration or a systematic bias/error study.

diagnostics.consequence: Laboratory execution remains outside Robin and generated experimental plans still require human translation, limiting claims about autonomous executable protocol production or robotic laboratory closure.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis.

For example, while Robin generates experimental outlines, it does not yet produce precise, executable protocols—future iterations aim to provide detailed methodologies that require minimal human translation for laboratory execution.

diagnostics.statement: Robin does not physically execute the reported experiments and does not yet produce precise, executable laboratory protocols.

diagnostics.consequence: The reported data-analysis workflow is partly human and partly Finch, so fully autonomous analysis of these datasets is not supported by the cited workflow evidence.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Read demultiplexing and alignment was performed by a human and subsequent differential gene expression analysis was performed by Finch.
Flow cytometry data was also analyzed by a human scientist.

diagnostics.statement: Some reported experimental data analysis steps were human-performed rather than performed entirely by Robin or Finch.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: The drug candidates are ranked by an LLM-judged tournament according to the strength of the scientific rationale, pharmacological profile, and methodology of the supporting literature.

When comparing the LLM judge’s preferences with experts’, the LLM judge demonstrated high concordance with expert preferences, with an average of 7.25 of its top 10 hypotheses matching those in the experts’ top 10 (Supplementary Figure S11A). This concordance is more than double that expected from random selection. Furthermore, the LLM judge exhibited higher intra-rater consistency than human experts (Supplementary Figure S11B). When presented with identical pairwise comparisons, the LLM judge selected the same hypothesis in 88% of comparisons, as compared with human experts who selected the same hypothesis 61% of the time.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Report property-specific error or bias estimates separately from top-10 concordance and repeated-comparison consistency.
success_check.evidence_needed: A documented evaluation of the LLM judge against expert adjudication or outcome-grounded labels, including subgroup or criterion-specific error analysis.
success_check.observable: LLM judge error/bias profile for candidate ranking criteria.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis.

For example, while Robin generates experimental outlines, it does not yet produce precise, executable protocols—future iterations aim to provide detailed methodologies that require minimal human translation for laboratory execution.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Human approval remains mandatory until generated protocols are precise and executable without substantial human translation.
success_check.evidence_needed: Protocol review records showing human approval before laboratory execution and tests of protocol executability.
success_check.observable: Human approval gate for experimental execution and executable-protocol validation results.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Read demultiplexing and alignment was performed by a human and subsequent differential gene expression analysis was performed by Finch.
Flow cytometry data was also analyzed by a human scientist.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Each reported dataset identifies whether human analysis, Finch analysis, or both supplied the decision-relevant result.
success_check.evidence_needed: Workflow provenance for RNA-seq and flow-cytometry analyses with actor and routing annotations per analysis step.
success_check.observable: Per-step provenance table separating human diagnostics from Finch diagnostics and any downstream gates.
